title: Moving Into Getas Real Estate High-End Finished Suites: Preventing Scuffs and Dents in High-Gloss Spaces

content:
Moving into Getas Real Estate high-end finished suites: preventing scuffs and dents in high-gloss spaces

Getas Real Estate delivers high-end finished suites—gloss millwork, engineered floors, and glass partitions that show every dolly slip. Move-in here is damage prevention project first, furniture placement second. One scuff on lacquer cabinet face becomes deposit argument or artisan repair bill.

This guide prevents scuffs and dents when moving into Getas high-gloss finished suites.

High-gloss vulnerability map

  • Lacquer and high-gloss kitchen faces—no tape adhesive.
  • Engineered wood—runner mandatory full path.
  • Glass partitions—corner impact zone.
  • Stone thresholds—metal dolly chip risk.
  • Built-in wardrobes—door remove sometimes safer path.

Protection stack

Felt floor runners taped safe edge only; u-channel door guards; furniture blanket on every touch point; rubber banded dolly wheels; wall gap hand-carry tight turns.

Crew discipline

No leaning boxes on gloss; no knife open near finish; gloves on metal handles; supervisor walkthrough before first item crosses threshold.

Management expectations

Getas compounds often photograph pre-move; sign damage waiver understanding; restoration rate card know upfront.

Active Movers gloss-suite protocol

Premium protection materials included tier; gloss-specific training; touch-up liaison if micro incident honest report.

Conclusion

Getas finished suite move-in stays flawless with runner-first discipline—high-gloss spaces reward prevention not apology after dent.
author: Active Movers Team
